steps.title: Decode the 'no plateau' finding
steps.content:
The crucial signal is that vitamin D levels show “no evidence of plateauing” as UVB reaches its highest local levels.

A plateau would mean vitamin D synthesis has hit its ceiling.

Since there's no plateau, the chimpanzees' synthesis capacity hasn't been reached—they would produce more vitamin D if exposed to higher UVB.

steps.title: Apply the finding to the choices
steps.content:
If vitamin D production would increase with more UVB, then under the current (lower) UVB in Norway, these chimpanzees are producing less vitamin D than their inherent capacity allows.

Choice C expresses exactly this: they synthesize less vitamin D than they are inherently capable of synthesizing.

Choices A (contradicting the lower UVB premise), B (speculating about adaptations), and D (discussing supplementation) don't follow from the findings.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
